headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Drops As Trump's War Comments Hit Stocks; Oil Surges (Live Coverage)
summary: The Dow Jones index fell Thursday as Trump warned Iran to "get serious soon" about a peace deal. Oil prices surged.

headline: ASX Stocks That May Be Trading Up To 46.2% Below Intrinsic Value Estimates
summary: As Australian shares experience a modest rise amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainties, investors are keenly observing the ASX 200's movements influenced by international tensions and domestic industrial actions. In such a fluctuating market, identifying stocks trading below their intrinsic value can present opportunities for those looking to capitalize on potential market inefficiencies.

headline: Dow Jones Futures Fall, Oil Prices Rise; Trump Warns Iran To 'Get Serious Before It Is Too Late'
summary: Futures fell while oil prices rose as President Trump warned Iran to "get serious before it is too late" about a deal. Space play Karman Holdings reported earnings late.

raw.headline: Dow Jones Futures Fall, Oil Prices Rise; Trump Warns Iran To 'Get Serious Before It Is Too Late'

headline: Lockheed Martin Stock: Is The Iran War Already Priced In?
summary: Lockheed Martin (LMT) stock hit an all-time closing high of $676.70 on March 2, pulled back to around $610, and recovered to trade near $624 by late March. Up roughly 40% over the past year, a move of this scale in a defense major demands a hard question: is it justified by fundamentals, or has the Iran war premium run its course.

headline: 1 S&P 500 Stock with Solid Fundamentals and 2 We Ignore
summary: The S&P 500 (^GSPC) is often seen as a benchmark for strong businesses, but that doesn’t mean every stock is worth owning. Some companies face significant challenges, whether it’s stagnating growth, heavy debt, or disruptive new competitors.
